full_text:
Automated Testing of Quantum Circuits: A
SWEBOK-Aligned Approach
Abstract— Testing quantum circuits presents unique challenges due to the probabilistic nature of quantum
computation and the exponential state space. In this paper we propose a framework for automated unit
and integration testing of quantum circuits implemented with Qiskit. We introduce mutation operators
specific to quantum gates (bit-flip, phase-flip, CNOT injection) and a test adequacy criterion based on
quantum state fidelity. Our evaluation on 30 open-source quantum algorithms shows that our mutation
score correlates strongly with defect detection rates, achieving 91% defect detection on seeded faults. The
framework integrates with standard CI/CD pipelines, enabling continuous quality assurance of quantum
software.
1. Introduction
Quantum software engineering (QSE) is an emerging discipline that adapts classical software engineering
practices to the quantum computing paradigm. As quantum hardware matures, ensuring the correctness of
quantum programs becomes increasingly critical. Classical software testing techniques do not transfer
directly because: (a) quantum states cannot be observed without collapsing them, (b) quantum programs
are non-deterministic, and (c) the Hilbert space grows exponentially with qubit count.
2. Background
We build on SWEBOK's Software Testing knowledge area, adapting concepts such as test levels (unit,
integration, system), test coverage criteria, and test adequacy. We also draw from quantum information
theory.
3. Methodology
Our framework defines quantum mutation operators and computes fidelity-based adequacy scores. We
implemented the framework as a pytest plugin.
4. Evaluation
Experiments on 30 Qiskit programs demonstrate 91% defect detection.
5. Conclusion
Automated quantum circuit testing is feasible using adapted classical techniques.
